### 🎯 4. Member ID Extraction for Leaked Databases

Is topic mein hum ek advanced OSINT technique sikhenge jisme hum target profile ka HTML source code inspect karke unka unique, hidden "Member ID" extract karte hain. Yeh specific ID baad mein third-party leaked databases (breached data dumps) mein target ke passwords ya personal info dhoondhne ke kaam aayega.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Maan lo tumhara naam "Rahul" hai, par RTO (Road Transport Office) tumhe tumhare driving license number (e.g., DL-12345) se jaanta hai. Agar tum apna naam badal bhi lo, tumhara License Number wahi rahega. LinkedIn ka "Member ID" wahi License number hai. Agar kisi hacker ke paas purana leaked database hai, woh wahan "Rahul" search nahi karega (kyunki hazaron Rahul hain), woh exact DL-12345 (Member ID) search karega taaki direct target ka leaked password mil jaye.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Member ID Extraction is the process of parsing the raw HTML source code of a LinkedIn profile to uncover the unique, persistent alphanumeric identifier assigned to an account. This static ID is heavily utilized by attackers to cross-reference targets within massive breached data sets and leaked databases.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Member ID ek unique hidden number hota hai jo target ki profile ke page source mein chhipa hota hai. Hum usko nikalte hain taaki leaked databases (jahan companiyon ka chori hua data hota hai) mein target ka password aur email dhoondh sakein.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Target apna custom LinkedIn URL (`/in/cool-hacker-99`) ya apna display name kabhi bhi change kar sakta hai. Aise mein breached databases mein usse trace karna lagbhag impossible ho jata hai.
* **Solution:** Member ID kabhi change nahi hota (static hota hai). Data breaches (jaise 2012 ya 2021 ka LinkedIn breach) mein data hamesha Member ID ke against index hota hai. Is ID se tum exact person ka leak dhoondh sakte ho.
* **What breaks if we don't know this?** Tum leaked databases mein target ka naam search karte rahoge aur false positives (kisi aur bande ka data) gather kar loge, jisse poora attack vector fail ho jayega.
* **✅ Kab use karo (Use in engagement when):** Jab credential stuffing (purane leaked passwords naye systems pe try karna) attack plan kar rahe ho, aur target ka leaked password dhoondhna ho agle phase mein.
* **❌ Kab mat karo / Alternative prefer karo:** Jab target ka account bilkul naya ho (kuch din purana), toh woh kisi purane breached database mein nahi hoga, toh wahan ID extract karne ka faida nahi.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

```html
<!-- Notepad mein HTML Source Code ka ek chhota hissa -->
"member:urn:li:member:123456789","firstName":"Rishi","lastName":"Kabra"

```

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

(1) **Architecture Data Binding:** Web applications (jaise LinkedIn) data render karne ke liye JSON aur HTML blobs generate karte hain. Frontend URL (`/in/rishi-kabra`) sirf aesthetic hai; backend mein API calls `member:123456789` ke format mein hoti hain.
(2) **Source Code Scraping:** Jab hum "View page source" (target page ki raw coding dekhna) karte hain, toh humein woh raw API data dikh jata hai.
(3) **The Extraction Logic (Self ID Exclusion):** Ek profile page mein 50 se zyada baar `member:` shabd aata hai. Usme se mostly tumhara apna ID hota hai (kyunki tum logged-in ho). Attacker pehle apna ID identify karta hai, usko Notepad mein "replace with nothing" (delete) karta hai, taaki jo bacha hua naya ID mile, woh confirm target ka hi ho.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step Target ID Extraction Flow:**

```text
# OSINT Web Workflow | Browser & Text Editor (Notepad)
1  # Step 1: Login to your sock-puppet (fake) LinkedIn account.
2  # Step 2: Target ki profile (e.g., Rishi Kabra) par jao. Page par Right-click karo aur "View page source" select karo.
3  # Step 3: Raw source code khulega. `Ctrl+A` (select all) aur `Ctrl+C` (copy source code) press karo.
4  # Step 4: Apna Notepad (ya koi text editor) kholo aur pura code wahan paste karo.
5  # Step 5 (Find Your Own ID): Notepad mein `Ctrl+F` dabao aur search karo: `member:`
6  #       Pehela ID jo milega (kuch matches aayenge), woh most likely tumhara apna (sock-puppet ka) ID hoga kyunki page pe tumhara session active hai.
7  # Step 6 (Exclude Member ID): Apne ID number ko copy karo. Notepad ke "Replace" function mein jao.
8  #       Find what: [Tumhara ID Number]
9  #       Replace with: [Kuch mat likho, blank chhod do] (replace with nothing)
10 #       Click "Replace All". Ab tumhara ID poore source code se hat gaya.
11 # Step 7 (Find Target ID): Wapas `Ctrl+F` karke `member:` search karo. Ab jo naya number milega, woh definitely target ka unique Member ID hoga!
12 #       Is ID ko save kar lo for the next phase.

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
Target Ka Member ID: 45781290 (Yeh number aage chal ke breached DBs mein query param banega).

```

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense (Dual Perspective)

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** Attacker target ka Member ID collect karke dark web dumps (leaked database files) mein search karta hai. Agar us member ka password 2016 ke breach mein leak hua tha, attacker us purane password ko modify karke (jaise `Pass@2016` ko `Pass@2024` banake) target ke corporate login pe try karta hai (Password Spraying).
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** Users apne Member ID ko hide nahi kar sakte, lekin woh password reuse attack se bachne ke liye MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) aur password manager use kar sakte hain taaki agar leaked database mein purana password mil bhi jaye, toh naya system compromise na ho.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Ek external network penetration test ke dauran, Red Team ko VPN portal access karna tha. Unhone LinkedIn OSINT se company ke IT manager ka profile khola. "View page source" karke uska Member ID nikala. Phir ek underground "Collection #1" leaked database mein us Member ID ko query kiya. Wahan se manager ka ek purana leaked password nikla: `Winter2019!`. Attacker ne VPN portal pe login kiya aur password ko `Summer2023!` guess karke try kiya. Password chal gaya aur Red Team ko corporate network mein initial shell mil gaya. Instructor ne exactly isliye Member ID sikhaya hai — to prepare for the leaked database phase!

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Source code me pehla `member:` ID dekh ke usko target ka ID maan lena.
* **🤦 Why:** LinkedIn page source mein bohot saare tracking elements hote hain jo current logged-in user (tumhare) ID se tied hote hain. Pehla ID hamesha tumhara apna hota hai.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha pehle apna ID dhundho, usko "exclude" (replace all with nothing) karo, phir target ka ID dhundho.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar bina soche pehla ID le liya, toh agle section mein leaked database mein tum apna khud ka password dhundh rahe hoge target ki jagah!

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya target ko pata chalega ki maine uske page ka source code dekha?"**
* **Galat soch:** Right-click > View page source se target ke paas alert jata hai.
* **Actually:** "View page source" ek completely local, client-side action hai. Tumhare browser ne jo code download kiya hai, tum bas usse padh rahe ho. Isse koi naya request server par nahi jata aur koi alert trigger nahi hota.
* **Prove karo:** Apna internet disconnect karo (WiFi off karo). Ab kisi pehle se khule hue page pe View page source karo — woh phir bhi khul jayega kyunki woh locally saved hai.


* **Confusion 2 — "Main directly URL se ID kyu nahi utha leta?"**
* **Galat soch:** URL (`[linkedin.com/in/rishi-123](https://linkedin.com/in/rishi-123)`) mein jo 123 hai wahi ID hai.
* **Actually:** URL mein dikhne wala ID vanity URL (cosmetic) hai. Backend database ID (`urn:li:member:...`) strictly source code ke andar ek JSON API call ke andar hidden hota hai.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`Error: Replace All karne ke baad bhi "member:" search par koi naya ID nahi mil raha`**
* **Root Cause:** Tumne sirf number (jaise 12345) replace kiya, par tag nahi, ya phir tum galat jagah search kar rahe ho.
* **Fix:** Notepad mein case-sensitivity band karo aur exactly `member:` colon ke sath search karo. Ek profile mein hamesha target ka ID kam se kam 10+ jagah zaroor hota hai.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Tool vs Tool / Technique vs Technique)

| Feature | Custom Vanity URL (`/in/rishi`) | Internal Member ID (`member:12345`) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Changeability** | User easily change kar sakta hai | System generated, static, never changes |
| **Visibility** | Front-end UI pe publicly visible | Sirf backend HTML Page Source mein hidden |
| **Breach Value** | Low (Breaches IDs pe index hote hain) | Extremely High (Primary key for leaked databases) |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Reconnaissance / OSINT
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Weaponization Prep (Bridging to Breach Data)
🔗 **This connects to:** Credential Harvesting (Leaked Database Hunting)
🔄 **Flow:** Open Target Profile -> View Page Source -> Copy to Notepad -> Search `member:` -> Exclude your own ID (Replace All to blank) -> Find Target Member ID -> Use in Leaked Databases.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow)

```text
[LinkedIn Profile View]
         |
    (View Page Source)
         v
[Raw HTML & JSON Data] -> Contains Both IDs
         |
      (Ctrl+A / Ctrl+C)
         v
[Notepad Text Editor]
  |-- Step 1: Find "member:" (Gets YOUR ID: 9999)
  |-- Step 2: Replace "9999" with "" (Nothing)
  |-- Step 3: Find "member:" again
         v
[Target Member ID: 12345]  =======> (Next Phase) Search in Leaked Databases

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q:** Why do we need to extract the Member ID from the page source rather than just using the person's name to search in a leaked database?
* **A:** Names common ho sakte hain aur targets apne names ya custom URLs change kar sakte hain. Lekin Member ID database ka primary key hai jo kabhi change nahi hota. Data breaches Member ID ke base par index hote hain, isliye accurate result (passwords/emails) ke liye yeh ID nikalna zaroori hai.
* **Q:** Why do we use the "replace with nothing" technique in Notepad when extracting the target's Member ID?
* **A:** Page source mein hamara apna logged-in session ID bohot baar repeat hota hai. Apne ID ko "exclude" ya delete (replace all) karne se noise khatam ho jati hai, aur jo bacha hua `member:` ID samne aata hai, woh definitely target ka hota hai.

### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Naam badal sakta hai, URL badal sakta hai, par Source code ka ⭐'member:' ID target ka pichha dark web ke leaked databases tak karta hai!"

### 🔑 18. Keywords Coverage Verification

# Section 14.5: Corporate Intelligence & Shell Companies

=====Section 14.5: Corporate Intelligence & Shell Companies [⚠️ Derived]=====
Is section mein hum corporate footprinting aur financial forensics ki deep dive karenge. Hum dekhenge ki kaise public business registries aur offshore leaks ka use karke kisi organization ki corporate hierarchy, uske hidden assets, aur shell companies (paper companies bina active business ke, aksar tax ya assets chhupane ke liye) ko uncover kiya jata hai.

---

### 🎯 1. Business Registries & Offshore Leaks [⚠️ Derived]

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki **OpenCorporates** (global open database of corporate entities) aur **ICIJ Offshore Leaks Database** (leaked offshore financial records ka search engine) ko use karke target executives ka hidden corporate network kaise map karein. Yeh technique LinkedIn OSINT se ek level upar hai kyunki yeh verified government records aur leaked dark-money data par based hai.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho tumhe kisi ke baare mein pata karna hai. LinkedIn uski "Dating Profile" hai jahan woh khud bata raha hai ki woh kitna acha hai. Lekin **Business Registry** uska "Aadhar/PAN Card detail" hai jo government ne verify kiya hai. Aur **Offshore Leaks** uski "Secret Swiss Bank Diary" hai jo kisi ne chura ke public kar di hai. Ek attacker sirf profile pe trust nahi karta, woh government records aur leaked diaries match karta hai asaliat (beneficial owner) janne ke liye.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Corporate OSINT involves leveraging official business registries and leaked financial databases to conduct corporate footprinting, identify beneficial owners, and map cross-border businesses and shell companies that executives use to obscure assets or liability.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Corporate OSINT matlab public aur leaked databases ka use karke company ke asli malik (beneficial owners) aur unki chhupi hui paper-companies (shell companies) ka pata lagana taaki poora corporate structure map ho sake.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Executives apni LinkedIn profile pe sirf wohi dikhate hain jo legal aur clean ho. Unke asli investments, tax havens mein chhupe assets, aur physical addresses hidden hote hain.
* **Solution:** Corporate intelligence humein registered address, directors list, aur corporate hierarchy (parent-child company structure) ka exact mapping deti hai.
* **What breaks if we don't know this?** Tum red teaming mein galat office branch mein physical pentest plan kar loge, ya spear-phishing ke liye galat "parent company" ka naam use karoge jisse target alert ho jayega.
* **✅ Kab use karo (Use in engagement when):** Jab financial forensics (financial fraud investigation) karni ho, ya high-profile executives (C-Suite) ke khilaf high-tier social engineering campaign design karna ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo / Alternative prefer karo:** Jab target ek entry-level ya mid-level employee ho (woh shell companies nahi banate). Us case mein basic social media OSINT prefer karo.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

```text
# OpenCorporates UI:
Company: XYZ Holdings Ltd | Status: Active
Directors: John Doe, Jane Smith | Registered Address: 123 Main St, London

# ICIJ Offshore Leaks UI:
Node Graph: [John Doe] ----(Shareholder of)----> [Alpha Shell Ltd (BVI)]

```

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

(1) **Data Aggregation:** **OpenCorporates** alag-alag countries ke official government registries se data scrape karke ek unified search engine banata hai. **SEC EDGAR filings** (US publicly traded companies ki mandatory financial reporting) bhi isme madad karti hain.
(2) **The Leak Mapping:** Jab **Panama Papers** ya **Pandora Papers** (massive offshore data leaks) public hue, toh International Consortium of Investigative Journalists (ICIJ) ne us data ko ek searchable node-graph mein index kar diya.
(3) **The Attack Flow:** Attacker pehle OpenCorporates se target ke legal naam aur addresses nikalta hai, phir un details ko ICIJ database mein cross-reference karke cross-border businesses discover karta hai.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation & Querying:**

```bash
# Browser | Corporate Footprinting Workflow
1  # Step 1: OpenCorporates.com par jao
2  # Query target company or executive:
3  Search: "Rishi Kabra"  # Target executive ka naam dalo
4  Filter: Jurisdiction -> "United Kingdom" (taaki false positives kam hon)
5  # View result: Find the company where target is listed as an officer.
6  # Extract: Registered address aur baki directors list note karo.
7  
8  # Step 2: OffshoreLeaks.icij.org par jao (Offshore Leaks Database)
9  Search: "Rishi Kabra"  # ICIJ search bar mein same verified name dalo
10 # Explore Nodes: Agar target data leaks (Panama/Pandora Papers) mein hai, toh ek visual graph khulega
11 # Extract: Check karo ki kya yeh 'Beneficial Owner' hai kisi tax haven (BVI, Cayman Islands) ki shell company ka.

```

```
# 📤 Expected Output:
Target executive ki officially linked companies aur agar usne koi tax haven mein assets chhupe hain, toh uska node-based connection map screen par dikhega.

```

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense (Dual Perspective)

**🔴 Attacker Perspective (Red Team):** Attacker is data ka use physical penetration testing ke liye (registered address pe jake) aur spear-phishing ke liye karta hai. Agar attacker ko ICIJ mein target ki offshore company mil gayi, toh woh "Panama Tax Authority" ban kar ek highly-credible phishing email bhej sakta hai jo target turant open karega panic mein.
**🔵 Defender Perspective (Blue Team):** Executives ko personal details hide rakhne ke liye law firms aur nominee directors (jo paper pe director hote hain par asli control kisi aur ka hota hai) ka use karna padta hai, jahan locally legal ho, taaki OSINT footprints kam hon.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Ek Red Team scope mein client ne apni ek subsidiary (child company) ka network test karne ko bola, par exact domain nahi diya. Attacker ne OpenCorporates mein parent company ka naam daala, wahan se "corporate hierarchy" map ki, aur target subsidiary ka exact legal name aur registered address nikal liya. ICIJ database se yeh bhi pata chala ki unka ek hidden offshore account tha, jiska reference de kar ek phishing mail finance team ko bheja gaya aur initial access mil gaya.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** OpenCorporates pe sirf naam search karna bina jurisdiction filter lagaye.
* **🤦 Why:** "John Smith" jaise naam duniya mein hazaron hain. Tumhe galat company ki details mil jayengi.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha jurisdiction (country/state) aur SEC EDGAR filings se data cross-verify karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Galat company map kardi toh tumhara poora engagement out-of-scope machines pe ho jayega, jo ki illegal ho sakta hai.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya Shell Companies hamesha illegal hoti hain?"**
* **Galat soch:** Shell company matlab 100% scam.
* **Actually:** Nahi, shell companies legitimate reasons ke liye bhi banti hain (jaise mergers, IP holding, ya international tax structuring). Par attackers ke liye yeh valuable hain kyunki inke connections hidden hote hain.
* **Prove karo:** OpenCorporates pe "Apple Inc" search karo, tumhe unki bohot si holdings milengi jo technically shell entities hain IP manage karne ke liye.


* **Confusion 2 — "OpenCorporates aur ICIJ Offshore Leaks mein kya farak hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono same type ke registries hain.
* **Actually:** OpenCorporates *White Data* hai (government ne officially public kiya hai). ICIJ Offshore Leaks *Grey/Black Data* hai (hackers ne law firms se chura ke public kiya hai, e.g., Panama Papers).



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`Error: OpenCorporates shows 10,000+ results for my target executive`**
* **Root Cause:** Naam bohot common hai.
* **Fix:** Target ka address, ya target ki known previous company ke naam ke sath usko filter karo taaki specific entity hi mile.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Tool vs Tool / Technique vs Technique)

| Feature | OpenCorporates | ICIJ Offshore Leaks | SEC EDGAR |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Data Source** | Government Registries | Leaked Law Firm Data (Panama Papers) | Mandatory US Filings |
| **Visibility** | Official & Public | Secret & Hidden | Public (US Public Cos) |
| **Use Case** | Find legit HQ & Directors | Find tax havens & shell entities | Find financial reports & structure |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Reconnaissance / OSINT
📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Phase 1 (Corporate Footprinting)
🔗 **This connects to:** Social Engineering Preparation & Physical Recon
🔄 **Flow:** Target Executive identified -> Search OpenCorporates -> Map Corporate Hierarchy & Directors -> Search Directors in ICIJ Offshore Leaks -> Discover Cross-Border Shell Companies -> Craft highly targeted pretext.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow)

```text
[Target: Rishi Kabra]
         |
  (OpenCorporates)
         |
         v
[Parent Co: Alpha Holdings Ltd]
  |-- Address: 123 London St.
  |-- Director 2: Jane Smith
         |
   (ICIJ Search)
         |
         v
[Offshore Data: Panama Papers]
         |-- (Beneficial Owner of) --> [Hidden BVI Shell Co.]

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q:** In an OSINT investigation, why would you cross-reference a target's details from OpenCorporates with the ICIJ Offshore Leaks database?
* **A:** OpenCorporates target ki official, public corporate footprinting (registered address, directors) provide karta hai. Is verified data ko hum ICIJ (Pandora/Panama Papers) mein search karte hain taaki target ke undeclared cross-border businesses, shell companies, aur hidden assets ka pata lagaya ja sake, jo fraud investigations ya complex spear-phishing scenarios mein critical hote hain.
* **Q:** What is the primary use of SEC EDGAR filings during reconnaissance?
* **A:** SEC EDGAR filings publicly traded US companies ke verified financial documents, corporate hierarchy, aur executive details extract karne ke liye use hote hain.

### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"LinkedIn pe log shareef bante hain, par ⭐OpenCorporates unki public kundli hai aur ⭐ICIJ Offshore Leaks unke chhupe huye ⭐shell companies (Panama Papers) ka sach ugalta hai."

# Section 15: Other Social Media OSINT



Tumhara Section 15 ka skeleton process ho raha hai. Is section mein hum target profiles ke deep OSINT (Open Source Intelligence — publicly available data se target ki information nikalna) techniques seekhenge. Hum third-party tools ke saath-saath manual DOM (Document Object Model — webpage ka HTML structure) aur Network extraction par focus karenge.



---

### 🎯 1. Snapchat OSINT Investigations

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki Snapchat public stories ko directly browser ke network traffic se manually kaise intercept karein, metadata timeline mapping kaise karein, aur target ke avatar profile se physical traits kaise extract karein.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Snapchat stories ek aisi nadi (river) ki tarah hain jiska paani (video/image) 24 ghante baad ghaayab ho jaata hai. Agar tum usme haath daal ke paani pakadne ki koshish karoge (screenshot/screen record), toh platform ko pata chal jayega. Lekin **Network Tab interception** aisi technique hai jisme hum nadi ke udgam (source pipe) par hi ek chhanni (filter) laga dete hain — paani seedha humari balti mein girta hai bina kisi ko pata chale.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Snapchat OSINT involves dynamically intercepting ephemeral media streams via browser developer tools (Network/Media filters) to bypass platform restrictions and extract raw media, coupled with physical profiling using Snapcode avatar traits and timezone correlation.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Snapchat pe jo public stories ghaayab ho jaati hain, unka raw server link browser ke background logs se nikal kar manually save karna, aur target ke bitmoji se uski physical appearance map karna.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/OSINT Investigator ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Target ki snapshot account timeline ephemeral (temporary) hoti hai. Agar evidence record nahi kiya, toh woh hamesha ke liye delete ho jayega.
* **Solution:** Manual download method se hum original file (with highest quality aur metadata tracking) capture karte hain, jo legal reporting ke liye zaroori hai.
* **What breaks?** Agar tum sirf online downloaders pe depend karoge, aur woh site down ho gayi, toh tumhare paas data capture karne ka koi tareeqa nahi bachega.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target footprinting karni ho, "evidence in a court" (legal proof) ke liye high-fidelity media capture karna ho, ya cross-platform profiles (Facebook context, LinkedIn reference) verify karne ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Private accounts par — yeh technique strictly "public stories" aur spotlight feature stream ke liye hai.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

```text
Browser Developer Tools -> Network Tab -> Media Filter
Name                              Status   Type       Size      Time
----------------------------------------------------------------------
media.mp4?story_id=xyz...         200      media      2.4 MB    120ms
snapcode_image.png                200      png        45 KB     40ms

```

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack Flow)

1. **Request Initiation:** Jab tum target ka URL (jaise `Rishi Kabra 132` [unclear username handling] ya `Rishi Cobra 132` [unclear]) visit karte ho, toh browser Snapchat server se video stream URLs request karta hai.
2. **Network Interception:** Browser developer tools ka network tab in requests ko background mein log karta hai.
3. **URL Substitution:** Avatar customization ko analyze karne ke liye hum resource link substitution (URL parameters change karna) karte hain taaki different sizes ka snapcode image ya avatar indicators fetch kar sakein.
4. **Time Translation:** Server se jo time aata hai woh UTC time parameters mein hota hai. Target ki physical location (jaise Chennai, India) ke hisaab se hum usse Kolkata local time mein convert karte hain.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Flow)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Manual Download Method):**
Snapchat website shutdown ya third-party blockages ko bypass karne ke liye yeh approach aani hi chahiye.

1. Target ki profile biography URL open karo.
2. Screen pe **Right-click inspect** karo -> **Browser developer tools** open hoga.
3. Top menu se **Network tab** (jo saare background requests dikhata hai) par jao.
4. Filter section mein **Media filter** select karo.
5. Ab target ki video stream play karo. Neeche list mein ek media file appear hogi.
6. Us target video capture file par right-click karo aur **Open Image in new tab** (ya video in new tab) select karo.
7. Naye tab mein video play hone lagega -> **save video as** (⭐ instructor ne is step par emphasis kiya tha) click karke evidence save karo.

*(Instructor Emphasis: "learned how you can download public stories... manually and automatically, just in case the website got shut down")*

**Timeline Time Zone Mapping Example:**
Agar target ka metadata timeline event `4:20 UTC` dikha raha hai, aur tumhara target Chennai/Kolkata context mein hai:

* Hum **Savvy Time converter** (ek online timezone synchronization tool) use karte hain.
* 4:20 UTC ko daal kar Indian timezone (Kolkata) check karo -> Result: **9:50 PM evening window**. Yeh exact publication tracking target ki physical routine batata hai.

**Automated Alternative:**
Agar manual extraction nahi karni, toh **Snapchat Story Downloader portal** (ek third-party website) use kar sakte ho. Bas URL paste karo aur video capture ho jayega.

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective (Red Team/OSINT):**
Attacker snapcode generation aur visual analysis framework use karke target ki physical indicators identification karta hai. Jaise: beard tracking (daadhi hai ya nahi), glasses detection (chashma phenta hai), ya long hair classification. Yeh details social engineering aur physical profiling mein kaam aati hain.
**🔵 Defender Perspective (Blue Team):**
Users ko apni profile public list se hatani chahiye. Platform administrators video stream URLs ko short-lived tokens ke sath secure karte hain (taaki link copy karke baad mein use na kiya ja sake).

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Ek red team engagement mein jahan physical intrusion scoped tha, team ko employee (target "Rishi Kabra") ki entry timing identify karni thi. Team ne target ki public story ko manually intercept kiya, Savvy Time se local timestamp mapping (9:50 PM) ki, aur uske Snapcode avatar se physical traits (glasses, beard) identify kiye taaki building lobby mein usse recognize kiya ja sake. Is technical extraction ko "evidence in a court" ki tarah reliable maana jata hai.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Sirf Snapchat Story Downloader jaisi sites par depend rehna.
* **🤦 Why:** Aisi sites frequently ban ya shut down ho jaati hain.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha Right-click Inspect -> Network -> Media tab wali manual source download technique aani chahiye.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar third-party tool down hai, toh tum crucial live evidence miss kar doge jo 24 hours mein delete ho jayega.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Snapchat video phone mein save nahi hoti toh browser mein kaise ho rahi hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Platform video ko directly app ke andar hi lock kar deta hai.
* **Actually:** Web version par browser ko video play karne ke liye server se video stream URLs fetch karne padte hain. Network tab unhi HTTP requests ko log karta hai.
* **Prove karo:** Network tab kholo, Media filter lagao aur koi bhi YouTube/Snapchat video play karo. URL seedha dikh jayega.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart

* **`[Network tab empty hai, koi media file nahi dikh rahi]`**
* **Root Cause:** Tumne Network tab tab khola jab video already load/play ho chuki thi.
* **Fix:** Network tab open rakho aur page ko hard refresh (Ctrl+F5) karo taaki fresh requests intercept ho sakein.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison

| Feature | Manual Network Tab Extraction | Snapchat Story Downloader |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Dependency | Zero (Sirf browser chahiye) | High (Website online honi chahiye) |
| Stealth | Completely invisible | Tool server log mein entry jaati hai |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

* ⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Scanning & Enumeration
* 📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Pre-engagement Reconnaissance
* 🔄 **Flow:** Target Identification -> Cross-linked location tracking (Chennai/Kolkata) -> Media Interception (Network Tab) -> Physical Indicators Extraction -> Reporting.

### 🎨 15. Concept Visualization

```text
[Target User] ---> Uploads Public Story ---> [Snapchat Server]
                                                    |
[OSINT Analyst]                                     v
 ├── Opens DevTools (Network Tab) <---- Server streams video data
 ├── Filters by "Media"
 ├── Right-clicks intercepted .mp4 URL
 └── "Save Video As" (Permanent Archive)

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q:** How do you determine a target's precise local time of activity if a social media API only returns UTC values?
* **A:** Hum metadata tracking se UTC value nikalte hain (jaise 4:20 UTC), aur account context (locations cross-referencing jaise Chennai) use karke Savvy Time converter ke through local time (9:50 PM Kolkata time) mein translate karte hain.

### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Snapchat ki memory temporary hai, par Network Tab ki aankhein permanent hain."

### 🎯 2. TikTok Metadata Scrape & Timeline Analysis

Is topic mein hum TikTok ecosystem ke deeply hidden backend objects, JSON payload processing nodes, aur video IDs se exact Unix timestamps nikalne ki advanced forensic techniques decode karenge.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Maan lo tum ek branded shirt khareedte ho. Bahar se sirf shirt dikhti hai, par agar tum shirt ke andar wali choti si white tag (Universal Data for rehydration object) ko dhyan se padho, toh usme factory ka location, manufacturing date, aur kapde ka code (metadata) sab likha hota hai. TikTok pe bhi video bahar se normal lagti hai, par HTML inspector se hum us "hidden tag" ko nikal kar poori history padh sakte hain.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** TikTok metadata scraping involves parsing DOM elements to extract the `__UNIVERSAL_DATA_FOR_REHYDRATION__` script node, converting it to JSON, and decoding the 64-bit Snowflake Video ID structure (specifically extracting the first 32 binary bits) to compute the exact Unix epoch creation timestamp.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** TikTok video page ke HTML code mein chhupe huye data block (JSON payload) ko nikalna aur video ID ke numbers ko binary mein convert karke video upload hone ka exact second (Unix timestamp) pata lagana.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** TikTok UI par sirf "2 days ago" ya "10-21" likha hota hai. Court evidence ya forensic incident response mein aisi vague timeline invalid hoti hai.
* **Solution:** Video ID math computation aur HTML source grid extraction se exact second (exact publication tracking) aur corporate country parameters verification nikali jaati hai.
* **What breaks?** Bina iske, OSINT reports court-ready nahi ban sakti.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target account (from 1.3 billion active users) ka origin trace karna ho (jaise Ireland jurisdiction verification), ya bulk archive automation setup karni ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Agar normal video content dekhna hai toh yeh over-engineering hai.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

```json
// Firefox Platform Viewer output (JSON schema processing)
"ItemModule": {
  "7014603310034603270": {
    "author": "target_user",
    "region": "IE",  // Corporate country parameter
    "Avatar Larger": "https://p16-sign-va.tiktokcdn.com/...jpg"
  }
}

```

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Epoch Calculation & Rehydration)

1. **The Video ID Structure:** TikTok ek unique video ID (e.g., 19 digits) generate karta hai. Yeh decimal id random nahi hai!
2. **Epoch Calculation Logs (Math):**
* Step A: Decimal id ko Binary mein convert karo (decimal to binary operations).
* Step B: Us lambe binary string mein se sirf **first 32 digits isolation** (shuru ke 32 bits nikal lo).
* Step C: Un 32 binary bits ko wapas Decimal mein convert karo (binary to decimal computation).
* Step D: Jo number aayega, woh **Unix timestamp format** (1970 se ab tak ke seconds) hota hai. Ise epoch converter se padh lo!
* *Note:* October 2nd 2021 threshold ke baad TikTok ne id generation mechanism adjust kiya tha, par base math valid rehta hai.


3. **Rehydration:** Server page load fast karne ke liye poora database fields parsing ek `Universal Data for rehydration` naam ke tag mein bhej deta hai jisme source language configurations aur invisible profile artifacts execution hota hai.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Flow)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Rehydration Script Parsing):**
Instructor Note: *"reason why I'm showing you how to download a TikTok video manually is because in case this website got shut down, you'll still be able to download"*

1. Browser mein elements inspector panel kholo (Right click -> Inspect).
2. Structure hierarchy map ko scroll down karo aur HTML comment identifier string ke pass `Universal Data for rehydration` wala script element code dhundo.
3. Us block ke andar click karke inner code buffer string (lamba text) copy karo.
4. Use ek Notepad string block storage mein paste karo.
5. Uski file extension assignment `.json` rakh kar save karo (e.g., `data.json`).
6. Is `.json` file creation ko Firefox platform viewer mein open karo (Firefox JSON tree ko automatically sundar dikhata hai).
7. "Expand All" select karo. Yahan tumhe target ka active follower data arrays, profile image export link (Avatar Larger reference link), aur region settings (jaise IE identifier node -> Ireland) mil jayenge.

**Automated Extraction Alternatives:**
Agar manually math ya DOM layout scanning blocks mein time nahi lagana:

1. **unfurl website analytics:** Video link daalo, yeh portal utility saara metadata nikal dega.
2. **ticker cc download script:** Ek automated CLI utility jo selective buffer stream replication aur site dependency elimination mein madad karti hai, jisse offline source replication ho sake.

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective (OSINT Analyst):**
Data verification records mein dynamic avatar configuration lookup karke target ki high-resolution display configurations mapping (Avatar Larger) nikalta hai. Isse facial recognition AI (quality enrichment pipelines) better result deta hai. Also, `region` parameter (e.g., IE) se user ka account creation region mapping hota hai.
**🔵 Defender Perspective (Blue Team):**
Developers aisi storage engine optimization tags ko obfuscate karte hain ya payload processing nodes ko backend API calls pe shift kar dete hain taaki direct HTML scraping block ho jaye.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

Threat intelligence teams state-sponsored misinformation campaigns ko track karne ke liye TikTok metadata validation logs use karti hain. Jab ek fake account claim karta hai ki woh USA mein hai, analyst script extraction models se json nikalta hai. Agar `region` parameter value `IE` (Ireland) ya koi aur corporate country parameters verification dikhata hai, toh location spoofing catch ho jaati hai. Court reports ("evidence in a certain court") ke liye video ID epoch decoding immutable proof provide karti hai.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** External link tracking ke liye sirf Unfurl ya Ticker CC par depend rehna.
* **🤦 Why:** Automated extraction frameworks APIs pe based hote hain. API rate limit hui toh investigation ruk jayegi.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Manual right-click context tracking aur DOM tree traversal (blank space inspection framework) ki mastery honi chahiye.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Inability to perform profile validation sequences during critical active follower tracking operations.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya main video URL copy karke exact upload time nikal sakta hu?"**
* **Galat soch:** URL mein sirf user aur video ID hota hai, upload time nahi.
* **Actually:** Woh video ID hi apne aap mein upload time chhupaye hue hai! TikTok "Snowflake ID" format use karta hai. Video node parameters mein maujood ID ka binary translation exact Unix time deta hai.
* **Prove karo:** Koi bhi TikTok video URL ka ID lo, usse online Snowflake ID decoder mein daalo. Woh tumhe exact second bata dega!


* **Confusion 2 — "JSON file save karke Chrome engine testing mein kyun nahi dekhte, Firefox kyun?"**
* **Galat soch:** Chrome aur Firefox backend state parse karne mein same hain.
* **Actually:** Firefox ke paas ek built-in native JSON payload tracking and formatting engine hai jo nested arrays ko bina extension ke properly style karta hai. Chrome mein extra plugin chahiye hota hai.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart

* **`[Firefox viewer shows raw text instead of structured JSON tree]`**
* **Root Cause:** Copy karte waqt HTML elements ya `<script>` tags bhi string ke andar aa gaye hain.
* **Fix:** Notepad mein jao aur string ke start ka `<script id="__UNIVERSAL_DATA_FOR_REHYDRATION__">` aur end ka `</script>` hatao. File sirf curly braces `{ ... }` se start aur end honi chahiye.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison

| Extraction Model | Manual Rehydration Parsing | Automated Ticker CC / Unfurl |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Speed | Slow (Requires Notepad text manipulation) | Fast (One-click execution) |
| Evidence Quality | Forensically sound, verifiable raw source | Reliant on 3rd party parser integrity |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

* ⚔️ **Attack Phase:** Reconnaissance / OSINT
* 📍 **Kill Chain Position:** Information Gathering & Evidence Preservation
* 🔄 **Flow:** Target Element Node Parsing -> Universal Data Selection -> Payload Processing -> Target Script Extraction Models -> Telemetry Data Validation (Location/Epoch).

### 🎨 15. Concept Visualization

```text
[TikTok HTML Page]
 └── <html>
      ├── <head>
      └── <body>
           └── <script id="__UNIVERSAL_DATA_FOR_REHYDRATION__">
                 {
                   "ItemModule": {"VideoID": "70146..."},
                   "UserModule": {
                         "Region": "IE", [<- Ireland jurisdiction verification]
                         "Avatar": "Avatar Larger link" [<- Pixel resolution optimization blocks]
                   }
                 }
           </script>

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q:** Explain the mathematical process used to extract an exact timestamp from a TikTok Video ID.
* **A:** Video ID (jo unique video identifier hota hai) ko decimal to binary operations ke through convert kiya jata hai. Phir hum usme se first 32 digits isolation karte hain. In binary bits ki binary to decimal computation hoti hai, jo seedha Unix timestamp format deta hai (seconds since 1970).

### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Universal Rehydration object TikTok ka black box hai — ID se time nikalta hai, JSON se location."

# Section 16: Username OSINT - Tracking Online Identities


Yeh raha Section 16 ke pehle 2 topics ka detailed, exam-ready 18-point breakdown. Isme humne Username OSINT aur Search Engine Dorking ko cover kiya hai. Since baki topics (Credentials & CLI automation) kaafi deep hain, main unhe next part mein cover karunga taaki depth compromise na ho.

---

### 🎯 1. Username Collection & Search Engine Dorking

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki target ke raw usernames collect karke, Google dorks (advanced search operators) ki madad se unke hidden profiles, email exposure, aur phone records ko kaise track kiya jata hai.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho tumhe ek bheed bhare mele mein apne dost "Rahul" ko dhundhna hai. Agar tum sirf "Rahul!" chillaoge, toh 50 log mud kar dekhenge (yeh normal Google search hai). Lekin agar tum mike par bolo: "Mujhe woh Rahul chahiye jisne laal shirt pehni hai AUR chashma lagaya hai, MINUS woh Rahul jo Delhi se hain" — toh tumhe seedha apna dost mil jayega. **Google Dorking** bilkul yahi karti hai — search engine ko exact filters dekar kachra (irrelevant results) hata deti hai.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Username OSINT combined with search engine dorking involves leveraging boolean operators and specific search parameters to query a search engine's index, isolating precise endpoints, profiles, or data leaks associated with a target's online aliases.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Target ke usernames ko Google ke advanced filters (`inurl:`, `OR`, `-`) ke saath search karna taaki sirf wahi pages dikhein jahan target ka profile actually exist karta hai.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Ek target ke naam ya username par hazaron useless search results aate hain. Manual verification mein ghanto waste hote hain.
* **Solution:** Dorking se hum straight unhi indexed URLs tak pahunchte hain jahan sensitive data ya hidden profiles public hain. Target filtering asaan ho jati hai.
* **What breaks?** Agar dorking nahi aati, toh tum kabhi bhi target ka hidden Quora ya old blog account nahi dhundh paoge jahan usne apni personal info (emails, tech stack) leak ki ho.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Recon/Discovery phase ke bilkul start mein jab target ke kuch initial handles mil gaye hon aur unki profile correlation karni ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Agar target ka naam bohot generic hai (jaise "John Smith") aur tumhare paas koi specific handle ya location validation nahi hai, toh dorks bhi false positives denge.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Google search bar mein ek lambi query type ki hui dikhegi, aur results mein sirf 3-4 highly relevant links dikhenge target ke Quora, Tableau, ya Twitch account ke, bajaye 10 lakh random results ke.

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

1. **Target Identification:** Tumhare paas kuch initial handles hote hain (e.g., `_RK 132`, `Rishi Raj Cobra`). Tum in sabko ek notes tracking `.txt file` mein save karte ho taaki track na kho jaye.
2. **Query Building:** Tum in handles ko boolean logic (e.g., `OR` operator) aur internal search operators (e.g., `inurl:`) ke saath mix karke ek query banate ho.
3. **Search Engine Index Query:** Jab tum Google par search karte ho, Google ka engine apne index (saved internet pages) ko chhan-ta hai aur sirf un URLs ko nikalta hai jahan exact match milta hai.
4. **Search Exclusion:** Jo identical names lekin galat log hain, unhe hatane ke liye tum minus (`-` operator) use karke irrelevant terms exclude karte ho.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

*(Google Search Bar Queries - GUI based)*

**Query 1: Basic Handle Combination (Using OR operator)**

```bash
# Google Search Bar
1  "Rishi Kabra" OR "Rishi Raj Cobra" OR "Rishi-Cobra" OR "Rishi cobra 132" OR "_RK 132"   # " " = exact phrase match; OR = inme se koi bhi ek search term page par ho toh result dikhao

```

```text
# 📤 Expected Output:
(Mix of relevant and irrelevant pages mentioning any of these exact terms)

```

**Query 2: Advanced Dorking (Targeting specific URL parameters & Excluding terms)**

```bash
# Google Search Bar
1  inurl:"rishi-cobra-2" OR inurl:"_RK 132" -cricket -news   # inurl: = search term URL ke andar hona chahiye (usually profile pages mein hota hai); -cricket = jin pages pe cricket likha ho unhe hata do (search exclusion); -news = news pages hata do

```

```text
# 📤 Expected Output:
https://www.quora.com/profile/rishi-cobra-2
https://public.tableau.com/app/profile/rishi.cobra.132
(Sirf exact profiles dikhengi, news aur cricket related kachra saaf ho jayega)

```

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense (Dual Perspective)

**🔴 Attacker Perspective (Red Team):** Attacker default blog accounts, hidden Quora profiles, ya Twitch streams dhundh kar target ki aadat, tech stack (jaise Linux, Java user hai ya nahi), email scraping aur phone records exposure nikalta hai. Yeh sab aage phishing campaigns mein use hota hai.
**🔵 Defender Perspective (Blue Team):**
Defender apne employees ki digital footprint mapping karta hai. Agar kisi purane platform par PII (Personally Identifiable Information) indexed hai, toh webmasters ko request bhej kar URLs ko Google search engine index se de-index (remove) karwata hai.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

**Red Team Social Engineering:** Engagement ke dauran, target "Rishi" ki details LinkedIn se nikali gayi. Dorking se uska ek purana Tableau account aur Quora profile mila. Quora par usne bataya tha ki woh specific chess games pasand karta hai aur uski location validation bhi wahan se confirm hui. Ab Red Team ne usko ek highly targeted spear-phishing email bheja — "Chess Tournament in your city" jisme ek malicious payload tha.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Unique usernames ko dimaag mein ya browser tabs mein khula rakhna, notes track na karna.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners sochte hain woh sab yaad rakh lenge.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Sabse pehle ek `.txt file` banao aur wahan saare raw handles copy-paste karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar track loose hua, toh tum footprinting phase mein aage ke steps miss kar doge aur time waste hoga.
* **❌ Mistake:** `-` (minus) operator use kiye bina hazaron results page 10 tak scroll karke dekhna.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Alert fatigue aur missed critical endpoints. Hamesha search exclusion use karo.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Kya Google Dorks use karna illegal hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dorks se hum hack kar rahe hain Google ko.
* **Actually:** Nahi. Tum sirf Google ke diye gaye search parameters use kar rahe ho publicly available data ko filter karne ke liye. Yeh 100% legal aur open OSINT hai.
* **Prove karo:** Google ke official support page par "refine web searches" search karo, Google khud in operators ko sikhata hai.


* **Confusion 2 — "`inurl:` aur `site:` mein kya fark hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono same website filter karte hain.
* **Actually:** `site:quora.com` sirf Quora ke andar dhundhega. `inurl:rishi` poore internet par un URLs ko dhundhega jinke link mein "rishi" word aata hai, chahe woh koi bhi site ho.


* **Confusion 3 — "`AND` aur `OR` operator mein confusion."**
* **Galat soch:** `OR` lagane se sab kuch ek sath milta hai.
* **Actually:** `OR` ka matlab hai "Koi ek bhi mil jaye toh dikhao" (results badh jayenge). `AND` (ya default space) ka matlab hai "Dono hone compulsory hain" (results kam ho jayenge).



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[Google shows "Unusual traffic from your computer" / CAPTCHA]`**
* **Root Cause:** Tumne bohot saari complex dork queries (inurl, site, OR) bohot jaldi jaldi lagatar run ki hain. Google ko lagta hai tum automated bot ho.
* **Fix:** CAPTCHA solve karo, apni search frequency thodi slow karo, ya VPN use karke IP change karo.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Tool vs Tool / Technique vs Technique)

| Feature | Manual Search (Names) | Google Dorking (`inurl:`, `OR`, `-`) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Precision | Very Low (hazaron false results) | Very High (exact matches) |
| Speed to find target | Slow (scrolling pages) | Fast (Top 5 results mein answer) |
| Used for | General OSINT | Advanced Footprinting / Target Filtering |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

* **⚔️ Attack Phase:** Scanning & Enumeration (Initial Footprinting)
* **📍 Kill Chain Position:** Recon/Discovery Phase.
* **🔗 This connects to:** Phishing campaign generation, Password breach lookups.
* **🔄 Flow:** Scrape raw handles (Twitter/LinkedIn) → Master matrix file (.txt) mein store karo → Deploy boolean queries on Google → Apply Minus (`-`) operator for filtering → Append discovered knowledge (Quora/Tableau) to report.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow / Network Topology)

```text
[Raw Target Data] 
  |-- "Rishi Kabra"
  |-- "_RK 132"
         |
         v
[Google Search Engine + Dorks]
  Query: inurl:"_RK 132" -cricket
         |
    +----+----+
    |         |
[Filtered]  [Excluded]
Quora       News Articles
Tableau     Cricket Blogs
Twitch

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: Target profiling mein Google Dorks ka primary use kya hai?**
* **A:** Publicly indexed pages se target ke hidden accounts ya exposed PII ko isolate karna bina noise ke. Operators jaise `inurl:` aur `-` (minus) use karke hum specifically profile pages nikal sakte hain aur irrelevant identically named persons ko exclude kar sakte hain.


* **Q: Agar ek handle se hazaron results aa rahe hain, toh unhe reduce kaise karoge?**
* **A:** Main search filtering use karunga. Target ki known attributes ke sath `AND` conditions lagaunga (jaise location ya company), aur jo cheezein target se related nahi hain unhe minus operator (`-term`) se search exclusion mein daal dunga.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Google dorks matlab search engine se exactly wahi nikalna jo chahiye, kachra (aur same naam wale log) MINUS karke."

### 🎯 2. People Search Engines & Online Lookup Tools

Is topic mein hum dekhenge ki manually dorking karne ke bajaye, automated engines ka use karke ek click mein 600+ websites par target ka username kaise scan kiya jata hai, aur visual validation se false positives ko kaise avoid karte hain.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Agar tumhe ek target ke bare mein pata karna hai ki uska account kahan-kahan hai, toh ek tarika hai ki Facebook pe jaake dhundho, fir Twitter pe, fir Instagram pe (manual dorking). Doosra tarika hai "WhatsMyName" jaise tool ka use karna — yeh ek aisa 'Master Key' robot hai jisko tumne target ka naam diya, aur usne ek sath 600 alag-alag doors (websites) par knock karke check kiya ki kahan darwaza khulta hai.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** People search engines and multi-username scanners are automated OSINT aggregators that programmatically send HTTP requests across hundreds of social networks and web domains to determine if a specific username is actively registered.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Aise web tools jo ek username ko ek sath saikdon websites (jaise Chess.com, Flickr, Pinterest) par scan karke batate hain ki account kahan exist karta hai.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters

* **Problem:** Target ke "niche" ya obscure platforms (jaise Goodreads, Chess.com) dhundhna manual tarike se lagbhag impossible hai, kyunki log inke baare mein kahin link nahi karte.
* **Solution:** Multi-username engine in saari 633+ websites ko automated scan karke result samne rakh deta hai.
* **What breaks?** Bina in aggregators ke, target ke dark/hidden hobbies ka pata nahi chalta. Ek target LinkedIn par professional dikhta hai, par Chess.com ya Pixel Art par apne passwords recycle kar sakta hai.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target ka ek strong, unique handle (jaise `_RK 132`) mil jaye aur tum target ka complete digital footprint aur profile interest analysis karna chahte ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Agar username bohot common hai (jaise `john123`), toh tool hazaron websites par account dikhayega, par woh kisi aur ke honge (false positives).

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Web tools (jaise whatsmyname.app) par target ka username enter karne par ek dashboard open hoga. 633 websites ki list mein se jo green higlighted honge, unka matlab account exist karta hai. Wahan direct profile ka link aur `CSV/PDF` download ka button dikhega.

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

1. **Input Submission:** Attacker tool (e.g., Blackbird ya whatsmyname) mein target ka handle daalta hai.
2. **Concurrent Backend Crawling:** Tool backend mein simultaneously hundreds of URLs generate karta hai (e.g., `chess.com/member/rishi-cobra`).
3. **HTTP Status Check:** Tool wahan GET request bhejta hai. Agar `200 OK` (page exists) return hota hai, toh woh usse "Found" mark karta hai. Agar `404 Not Found` milta hai, toh "Not Found".
4. **Visual Avatar Validation (Crucial Manual Step):** Tool sirf yeh batata hai ki username mapped hai. Attacker manually us link par click karke dekhta hai ki kya YouTube avatar match kar raha hai baki profiles se, ya account creation timestamp target ki age se match kar raha hai.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (WhatsMyName App):**

1. Browser mein `whatsmyname.app` open karo.
2. Search box mein username daalo (e.g., `rishicobra`).
3. Filter option se **'All'** select karo taaki poori 633 websites scan hon.
4. Search button par hit karo aur results populate hone ka wait karo.
5. Report section mein jaake ⭐**PDF report generation** ya **CSV data dump** button click karke result save karo aur use apne notes workspace embedding mein use karo.

*(CLI equivalent with Blackbird tool - Python based)*

```bash
# Kali Linux | Blackbird Tool (OSINT Multi-username scanner)
1  python3 blackbird.py --username rishicobra   # blackbird.py = OSINT tool script; --username = flag to specify target handle; rishicobra = target's handle

```

```text
# 📤 Expected Output:
[+] Searching username: rishicobra
[+] Chess.com: FOUND (https://chess.com/member/rishicobra)
[+] Pinterest: FOUND (https://pinterest.com/rishicobra)
[+] Goodreads: FOUND (https://goodreads.com/user/show/rishicobra)
[+] Report saved as rishicobra.pdf

```

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** Aggregators se attacker ko Flickr profile, Pinterest pins, Medium tracker, Threads handle sab ek jagah mil jata hai. Agar Goodreads reading logs se pata chale ki target cybersec books padhta hai, toh attacker us target ko exploit karne ke bajaye bypass karna pasand karega.
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** Employees ko train kiya jata hai ki apne professional handles (e.g., corporate username) aur personal handles (e.g., Chess, Gaming) ko alag rakhein. Gravatar verification (jahan email se avatar link ho jata hai) ko publicly expose hone se rokein.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

**Bug Bounty Scope Profiling:** Red team scoping ke dauran, attacker ne target ka handle ID Crawl (`idcrawl.com`) aur Blackbird tool par run kiya. Tool ne target ka ek abandoned Chess.com history link aur Gravatar profile pull kiya. Gravatar account target ke purane, unpatched blog se linked tha, jahan se attacker ko aage chal kar admin panel access (RCE) mil gaya.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Tool ne result mein "Found" dikhaya aur beginner ne use direct report mein daal diya.
* **🤦 Why:** Tools sirf URL check karte hain, identity nahi.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** Hamesha manual **visual avatar validation** (kya photo same hai?) aur **profile age correlation** (kya dates sense banate hain?) verify karo.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar bina validation ke client ko report di, aur woh account kisi random insaan ka nikla, toh report reject ho jayegi aur credibilty khatam hogi.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Tool ne account 'Found' dikhaya, par link kholne par blank page aata hai."**
* **Galat soch:** Target ne account delete kar diya abhi.
* **Actually:** Yeh ek 'False Positive' hai. Kuch sites `404 Not Found` ki jagah custom `200 OK` page bhejti hain ("User not found" text ke sath). Tool HTTP 200 dekh kar usse 'Found' samajh leta hai. Verify manually!


* **Confusion 2 — "Kya mujhe idcrawl aur whatsmyname dono use karne padenge?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono same websites scan karte hain, ek kaafi hai.
* **Actually:** Dono ke data sources aur target social networks scan lists alag hoti hain. Comprehensive footprinting ke liye multiple aggregators use karna standard OSINT practice hai.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[WhatsMyName App stuck at 50% or missing known profiles]`**
* **Root Cause:** Ya toh tumhara browser request limits hit kar gaya hai, ya site ka backend API rate-limit ho gaya hai (too many requests).
* **Fix:** Page refresh karo, handle dobara dalo, ya Blackbird jaise CLI tool ka use karo jo proxies ya delay handle kar sakta hai.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Tool vs Tool / Technique vs Technique)

| Feature | ID Crawl (`idcrawl.com`) | What's My Name (`whatsmyname.app`) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Scope | Limited to popular social media & deep web searches. | Extensive (633+ global & niche websites). |
| Best for | Broad identity mapping (names + numbers + usernames). | Strict username to profile linkage on specific endpoints. |
| Output | Web interface results. | CSV / ⭐PDF report generation options. |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

* **⚔️ Attack Phase:** Scanning & Enumeration
* **📍 Kill Chain Position:** Reconnaissance (Mapping broad perimeter).
* **🔗 This connects to:** Password reuse attack (agar target ka purana profile breach database mein mile).
* **🔄 Flow:** Enter input vectors in automated lookup sites → Trigger concurrent backend crawling → Map discovered endpoints (Chess.com, Pinterest) → Verify timeline metadata (active/inactive state) → Pull CSV data dump and embed in notes workspace.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow / Network Topology)

```text
                       [WhatsMyName / Blackbird]
                              | (HTTP GET /username)
       +---------------+------+------+----------------+
       |               |             |                |
  [HTTP 200]       [HTTP 200]    [HTTP 404]       [HTTP 200]
(Match Found)    (Match Found) (Not Found)   (False Positive!)
 Chess.com        Pinterest     Facebook          Medium
       |               |                              |
[Avatar Validated] [Date Verified]            [Blank profile - discard]
       |               |
   [CSV Dump -> Notes Workspace Embedding]

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: OSINT mapping phase mein automated tools (like WhatsMyName) false positives kyun generate karte hain?**
* **A:** Automated tools HTTP status codes (like 200 OK) par rely karte hain. Agar koi website apne 'User not found' page ke liye 404 error code ki jagah custom 200 OK response bhejti hai, toh tool use ek active profile samajh leta hai. Isliye visual avatar validation mandatory hai.


* **Q: Multi-username scanner se target ke account creation timestamps check karne ka kya fayda hai?**
* **A:** Profile age correlation se hum target ki timeline identify karte hain. Agar target ek teenager hai lekin Pinterest pin dates 15 saal purani hain, toh woh false positive hai. Agar profile recent hai, toh woh active attack surface ho sakta hai.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"whatsmyname aur blackbird se 600 sites ek minute mein chhan maaro, par AVATAR aur AGE match kiye bina report mein link mat chipkao."

### 🎯 3. Credential Leaks & Breach Database Pivoting

Is topic mein hum seekhenge ki target ke usernames ko leaked databases aur breach records mein kaise search kiya jata hai. Is technique se hum passwords, cleartext phone digits, aur aisi hidden identities nikalenge jo public OSINT se kabhi nahi milti.

### 🐣 2. Simple Analogy (Hinglish)

Socho ek chor ne saare shehar ki chabiyon (keys) ka ek box chura liya hai. Tum us box mein apne target ke purane ghar ki chabi dhundhte ho. Ab target ne naya ghar le liya hai, par aalsi hone ki wajah se usne naye ghar ka lock bhi wahi purani chabi wala rakha hai. **Password Reuse Pivoting** yahi hai — ek purani hack hui website (jaise Zomato) se password nikalo, aur target ke naye/current accounts (like email ya bank) ko usse kholne ka try karo.

### 📖 3. Technical Definition

* **Precise English:** Breach database pivoting involves querying indexed dumps of compromised platforms to extract a target's historical authentication material (hashes, cleartext passwords, PII) and correlating this data across other platforms to identify password reuse patterns or hidden linkages.
* **Hinglish Simplification:** Hackers dwara leak kiye gaye purane databases mein target ka username search karke uska password, number ya email nikalna, aur us information ko aage attacks (pivot) ke liye use karna.

### 🧠 4. Why This Matters (Pentester/Red Teamer ke liye Zaroorat Kyun Hai?)

* **Problem:** Target ka email aur phone number OSINT mein completely private/hidden ho sakta hai. Target ko direct hack karna bohot mushkil hota hai.
* **Solution:** Target ke purane accounts (jaise gaming forums ya food apps) ki security weak hoti hai aur woh already hacked hote hain. Wahan se data nikal kar hum direct identity mapping kar sakte hain.
* **What breaks?** Bina leaked databases ke, agar target ka primary attack surface zero hai, toh tum dead-end hit kar jaoge.
* **✅ Kab use karo:** Jab target ka username mil jaye par contact info na ho, ya jab initial access ke liye valid password list (dictionary attack) banani ho.
* **❌ Kab mat karo:** Active corporate accounts par purane leak data ko blindly spray (brute-force) mat karo, account lockout trigger ho jayega.

### 🔍 5. Visual / Terminal Mein Kya Dikhega

Web tools (jaise dehashed) par dashboard dikhega jisme rows hongi: `Email | Username | IP | Password Hash | Cleartext Password`. Offline tools (Agent Ransack) mein ek text search interface dikhega jahan folders ke andar se text match ho raha hoga.

### ⚙️ 6. Under the Hood (Deep Dive — Attack/Defense Flow)

1. **Search Parameter Entry:** Attacker ek search portal (like Leak Peek ya Breach Directory) par target ka handle string text match ke liye daalta hai.
2. **Database Lookup:** Engine backend par millions of leaked records (e.g., Zomato dump, Twitter leak index) mein scan karta hai.
3. **Data Extraction:** Result mein target ka username, hashed password ya cleartext entry, aur partial details aati hain.
4. **Pivoting (The Chain Reaction):** Agar hash milta hai, toh attacker dhash lookup (hash reverse engine) se use cleartext mein convert karta hai. Phir us cleartext password ko dobara search karta hai (reuse analysis) taaki target ke woh accounts mil jayein jahan usne same password rakha tha par naam alag use kiya tha.
5. **Facebook ID Conversion:** Facebook leaks (jaise 2021 breach) username se search nahi hote. Attacker ko pehle Facebook profile se numerical unique identifier (Facebook ID) alna padta hai, phir us ID ko leak data mein search karke cleartext phone digits extract kiye jate hain.

### 💻 7. Hands-On — Runnable Example (Lab-Ready Commands)

**🛠️ Step-by-Step GUI Navigation (Agent Ransack - Offline Breach Search):**

1. Offline raw databases (jo tumne dark web ya torrent se download kiye hain) wale local repository folders open karo.
2. Selected directory par Right-click karo.
3. Show more options execute karo.
4. ⭐**Agent Ransack utility** (fast file search tool for large data) option select karo.
5. Search terms field mein targeting Facebook ID ya password string feed karke run karo.
6. Tool GBs of text files ko parse karke exact line dega jahan data exist karta hai.

*(CLI equivalent for parsing large local breach files)*

```bash
# Kali Linux | Grep (Standard Linux Text Search)
1  grep -rnw '/home/kali/Breaches/Facebook/' -e '1000456123987'   # grep = search tool; -r = recursive (saare folders mein); -n = line number dikhao; -w = exact word match; '/path/' = breach data folder; -e = pattern/Facebook ID to search

```

```text
# 📤 Expected Output:
/home/kali/Breaches/Facebook/India_Leak.txt:14502: 1000456123987, +919876543210, Rishi, Kabra, Male

```

### 🔒 8. Attack Surface & Defense

**🔴 Attacker Perspective:** Credential dumps passive reconnaissance ka sabse operational weapon hain. Attacker ko target ka active password pattern samajh aa jata hai (e.g., `CompanyName@2023`). Snapchat data breach ya Facebook leak mapping se seedha phone number aur personal relationships expose hote hain.
**🔵 Defender Perspective:** Blue team tools jaise HaveIBeenPwned ki API integrate karti hai active directory mein. Agar user apna naya password kisi purane leak data jaisa rakhta hai, toh system password reject kar deta hai.

### 🌍 9. Real-World Penetration Testing Use-Case

**Red Team Identity Mapping:** Engagement mein ek pseudonym string ⭐`Zeus` mila. Ise `the hashed` (dehashed) platform par run kiya gaya, toh BigBasket breach aur Trax Tangle leak saamne aaye. Trax Tangle leak mein target ka cleartext password `ZeusCobra99!` mila. Phir Breach Directory par is password string hash aur pattern ko reverse search kiya, jisse target ka primary corporate email address leak records mein mil gaya, jo phishing ke liye aage exploit kiya gaya.

### ⚠️ 10. Pentest Anti-Patterns & Common Mistakes (Beginner Traps)

* **❌ Mistake:** Sirf HaveIBeenPwned use karna.
* **🤦 Why:** Beginners ko lagta hai sab kuch ek jagah milega.
* **✅ The 'Pro' Way:** HaveIBeenPwned sirf batata hai ki hack hua hai (passwords hide karta hai). Offense ke liye `dehashed` ya `Leak Peek engine` use karo jo actual data dikhate hain.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Agar actual password string nahi dekhi, toh password reuse pattern nahi milega aur pivoting possible nahi hogi.
* **❌ Mistake:** Facebook database ko seedha username/naam se search karna.
* **⚡ Consequences:** Facebook leak unique ID par indexed hota hai. Naam se search karोगे toh offline raw databases mein false positives aayenge ya system crash ho jayega kyunki file size bohot bada hota hai.

### 🤔 11. Agar Dimag Ghoom Raha Hai? (Confusion Clarifier)

* **Confusion 1 — "Password Hash aur Cleartext mein kya fark hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Dono same hote hain, tool automatically password de deta hai.
* **Actually:** Cleartext matlab seedha padhne wala password (`admin123`). Hash matlab scrambled code (`21232f297a57a5a743894a0e4a801fc3`). Agar database hashed hai, toh tumhe pehle usse crack ya dhash lookup karna padega tabhi reuse kar paoge.
* **Prove karo:** MD5 generator website par `admin` likho, jo random text aayega woh hash hai.


* **Confusion 2 — "Kya breached passwords se seedha account login karna legal hai?"**
* **Galat soch:** Agar publicly internet pe pada hai toh main use kar sakta hoon.
* **Actually:** Unauthorized access HAMESHA illegal hai (chahe password leaked ho). Pentesting mein hum is password format ko "pattern analysis" ke liye use karte hain, ya authorized scope ke andar credential stuffing simulation karte hain.



### 🛠️ 12. Troubleshooting Flowchart (Tool/Exploit Issues)

* **`[Agent Ransack "Out of Memory" or Freezes]`**
* **Root Cause:** Tum 50GB ki text file search kar rahe ho aur tumhare computer ki RAM kam hai.
* **Fix:** File ko chhote chunks mein split karo (e.g., `split` command in Linux) ya regex search ko bohot specific banao.



### ⚖️ 13. Comparison (Tool vs Tool / Technique vs Technique)

| Feature | HaveIBeenPwned alternative (Defensive) | Dehashed / Leak Peek (Offensive) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Show actual password? | No (Sirf batata hai data leak hua hai). | Yes (Cleartext aur hashes dikhata hai). |
| Best for | Personal security check, Blue Team alerts. | Red Team OSINT, Password reuse pivoting. |
| Cost | Free API tier available. | Mostly paid/subscription based. |

### 🔄 14. Kill Chain & Attack Phase Flow

* **⚔️ Attack Phase:** Reconnaissance / OSINT
* **📍 Kill Chain Position:** Pre-Engagement / Information Gathering.
* **🔗 This connects to:** Credential Stuffing attacks, Password Spraying, Social Engineering.
* **🔄 Flow:** Run dynamic username parameters on search portals → Grab single identity asset (password chunk) → Execute secondary leak pivot lists → Morph handle to Facebook ID → Parse offline databases using Agent Ransack.

### 🎨 15. Visual Diagram (ASCII Art — Attack Flow / Network Topology)

```text
[Username: Zeus]
       |
       v
[Dehashed / Leak Peek Engine]
       |
       +--> BigBasket Breach (Hit) --> Hash: e10adc...
       |
       +--> Trax Tangle Leak (Hit) --> Cleartext: ZeusCobra99!
                                              |
                                              v
                            [Password Reuse Pivoting]
                                              |
                     (Search "ZeusCobra99!" back into Database)
                                              |
                                              v
                              [Target's Hidden Corporate Email Found]

```

### ❓ 16. Interview & Certification Exam Q&A

* **Q: Password reuse pivoting ka kya matlab hai aur yeh offensive operations mein kyun critical hai?**
* **A:** Jab target apna same password multiple sites par use karta hai, toh ek vulnerable site ka data leak attacker ko us password tak pahuncha deta hai. Attacker us leaked password ko target ke secure/corporate accounts ke against test karta hai, jisse bina naya exploit dhundhe direct foothold (initial access) mil jata hai.


* **Q: Facebook ke raw offline databases parse karte waqt 'unique identifier' ka kya role hai?**
* **A:** Facebook data leaks text format mein hote hain aur aam taur par usernames se accurately index nahi hote. Target ke profile se pehle numerical Facebook ID conversion karna padta hai. Phir us ID ko Agent Ransack jaise tools mein feed karke exact row milti hai jahan cleartext phone digits ya email store hote hain.



### 📝 17. One-Line Memory Hook

"Zeus ki chori huyi chabi (Trax Tangle leak) se uski corporate vault (reuse pattern) khol do."

### 🔑 18. Keywords Coverage Verification

```text
🔑 Keywords Coverage Check — Credential Leaks & Breach Database Pivoting
✅ Covered   : leaked databases, breach records, HaveIBeenPwned alternative, ⭐the hashed, dehashed, dhash lookup, string text match, Leak Peek engine, Breach Directory, Trax Tangle leak, BigBasket breach, Zomato dump, ⭐Zeus credential, password string hash, password reuse pattern, Twitter leak index, Snapchat data breach, cleartext phone digits, Facebook leak mapping, Facebook ID conversion, unique identifier, offline raw databases, ⭐Agent Ransack utility
⚠️ Mentioned but needs more depth : (none)
❌ MISSED    : (none)

```
